title: Customer Assistant(Early Shifts) - Aungier Street
description: **Summary** From our Head Office to the shop floor and everywhere in between our primary goal is to create and deliver a fantastic customer journey for everyone who shops at Lidl. **Customer obsessed, high energy and love learning every day?** If that sounds like you, earn €460\+ per week as a Customer Assistant on our standard 30 hour contract. You’ll work across different shift times, on varied tasks and with different colleagues. What does a normal day\* as a Customer Assistant look like? (\*there’s no such thing as ‘normal’ in retail but we’ve tried our best!) As a Customer Assistant you are required to sell alcohol, therefore you must be 18 or over to work in our store. **Shift Patterns:** * Morning shift: Starting at 5am or 6am with your team, you’ll be getting our award\-winning products fully stocked on the shop floor and our delicious bakery products ready for our customers to enjoy by opening time so they can Go Full Lidl! * Middle shift: Starting mid\-morning or early afternoon, you’ll make sure our stores are spotlessly clean, well presented and our products remain fresh, all while helping customers with queries on the shop floor and working on tills to minimise till queues * Late shift: From your start time in the afternoon, you’ll make sure our customers experience is top class by serving them with a smile while on the tills, restocking missing items and getting the store ready for the next day **What You'll Do** * Interacting with the customer in a pleasant, friendly and helpful manner * Ensuring stock loss controls are followed in all areas of the store * Maintaining store and external cleanliness and hygiene standards * Following freshness and rotation principles * Ensuring all waste is managed correctly * Assisting in the stock count process **What You'll Need** * A can\-do attitude and excellent customer service skills * The willingness to go the extra mile for our customers * To be responsible and reliable * To enjoy working in a fast\-paced, varied environment * A good team player * Preferably, previous experience in a customer facing role but this is not essential provided you have the right attitude **What You'll Receive** We offer a competitive and transparent salary system, that ensures pay equality across all positions at Lidl * €15\.40 rising to €17\.90 per hour after 3 years (supplementary pay outlined below) * Unsocial hours worked (12am to 7am) * 20 days holidays per annum pro rata * Company pension * Genuine opportunities for career development * Individuals recognised as high performing will be invited to join our Leadership Academy as part of your on\-going development * Circle K and Private Health Insurance discounts available for all employees * Bike to Work Scheme * Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network * Maternity \& Paternity Leave top up, Marriage Leave, Employee Assistance Programme **Year 1** * Basic Rate €15\.40 * €19\.25 (Unsocial Hours) * €23\.10 (Overtime/Sundays) * €30\.80 (Bank Holiday) **Year 2** * Basic Rate €16\.20 * €20\.25 (Unsocial Hours) * €24\.30 (Overtime/Sundays) * €32\.40 (Bank Holiday) **Year 3** * Basic Rate €16\.90 * €21\.13 (Unsocial Hours) * €25\.35 (Overtime/Sundays) * €33\.80 (Bank Holiday) **Year 4** * Basic Rate €17\.90 * €22\.38 (Unsocial Hours) * €26\.85 (Overtime/Sundays) * €35\.80 (Bank Holiday) Lidl is an equal opportunities employer. If you have any particular requirements, we will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process. We value diversity and inclusion in our workplace and want to ensure that all applicants have the same opportunity regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, age, disability, race, religious belief, political opinion or membership of the traveller community.

title: Customer Growth Associate, SBS Scaled, GCS (English, Spanish)
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * Experience in digital sales, client management, customer service, business development, customer outreach, marketing, consulting, digital advertising, or related field. * Ability to communicate in English and Spanish fluently to engage with clients in the region. **Preferred qualifications:** * Familiarity with Google Ads and online advertising, with Google Digital Workshop certification or equivalent experience. * Ability to build compelling narratives and utilize storytelling as a client engagement strategy. * Ability to influence stakeholders internally and externally to deliver impact. * Ability to own an advertising or media sales portfolio in Spain, and consistently overachieve quotas to drive growth. **About The Job** Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. You bring a passion for sales, knowledge of online media, and commitment to maximize customer success. You act like an owner, move with velocity through change, finding innovative and strategic ways to consistently deliver extraordinary and incremental outcomes for both Google and your customers. You build trusted relationships with customers, uncovering their business needs and translating them into powerful solutions to achieve their most ambitious goals. You achieve as a team with sellers, shape the future of advertising in the AI\-era, and make a real impact on the millions of companies and billions of users that trust Google with their most important goals. Google Customer Solutions (GCS) sales teams are trusted advisors and competitive sellers who maintain a relentless focus on customer success by bringing the best Google has to offer to small\- and medium\-sized businesses (SMBs), which are the backbone of our communities. As a member of our team, you’ll have the opportunity to work with company owners and make a real difference in their businesses by helping them grow. Together, we help shape the future of innovation for customers, partners, and sellers...and we have fun doing it. Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €60000 \- €61500 (EUR) \+ 30% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits **Responsibilities** Learn more about benefits at Google . * Serve as a trusted advisor to clients by building relationships with key customer decision\-makers and business owners to understand their business goals and digital marketing plans, and help empower their business with Google advertising. * Manage large accounts and identify opportunities where Google Ads can impact growth while tracking account growth and product adoption quarterly goals. * Conduct frequent phone consultations to evaluate campaign performance, make account recommendations, and mentor/influence business owners on digital marketing strategy. * Engage in essential activities to drive customer relationship growth (e.g., proactive engagement, campaign performance review, client meetings, pitches, research, and internal strategy building/execution). * Participate in continuous training throughout the program to develop professional and business skills. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Customer Service Assistant - Hartstown
description: **Job Title:** Customer Service Assistant **Department:** Retail **Reporting to:** Shop Manager **Location: Hartstown** **Role Purpose** You will be responsible for providing an outstanding customer experience for our customers through a helpful, friendly, and knowledgeable approach. The day to day of the role will be to assist the Shop Manager and team with the smooth running of the store ensuring high standards are maintained throughout the day whilst accepting and paying out bets and delivering excellent customer service. The role encompasses the duty of opening and closing the business and on occasion you may be required to manage the operations of the shop. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist the Shop Manager and Assistant Manager with the day to day running of the shop. * Step up and guide shop operations in the absence of the Shop and Assistant Manager including being responsible for: + Opening and closing the shop + Managing and guiding the shop team + Cash management procedures * You will have the opportunity to manage on occasion which will lead to further development opportunities. * Ability to combine working in a fast\-paced retail environment whilst undertaking training/on the job supports to continuously upskill and progress within the company. * Responsible for providing an outstanding customer experience for customers through a helpful, friendly and knowledgeable approach. * Accept bets as per procedure, accurately taking and processing customer bets through multiple channels which include but not limited to betting terminals and in shop transactions through the EPOS System in timely manner. * To act as a Brand Ambassador including introducing customers to the multi\-channel/digital journey. * Ability to work under pressure and remaining calm whilst constantly looking for ways to improve yourself and the shop. * Keeping and maintaining shop presentation standards to a high quality throughout your working day. * Ensure that all procedures (safety, compliance and shop operations) are always followed. * Passionate about working within a team environment. * Adhere to Social Responsibility duties. * To introduce customers to our many in shop and online offerings/products. **Knowledge, Skills And Experience** Essential Skills and Experience * Customer service focused * Operate with trust and integrity * Flexibility as business operates 7 days a week * Be adaptable to shop environment * Great attention to detail and strong numeracy skills * An enthusiastic people person who can work on own initiative and as part of a team * A keen interest in Sporting industry/events and being confident to build rapport and relationships with customers. * Ambitious and driven to progress within the company * To promote the company brand and be results focused * Excellent communication skills * Problem solve and think creatively * Be organised, reliable and dependable * A proven good cash handing approach Desirable Skills and Experience * Industry/Retail experience is welcomed but not essential as full training provided to expand your knowledge base on all things sport and betting related. BoyleSports are an equal opportunities employer, and we welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons.

title: Customer Success €30k-€60k salary + commission
description: Salary: € 30,000 to € 60,000 base salary, plus commission or performance bonus Level: Entry level Open to: Graduates, non\-graduates and career changers Join Tangent’s commercial team Tangent is looking for ambitious entry\-level talent to join our commercial team and help more companies hire talented people from socially diverse backgrounds. This is a role working directly for Tangent. You will help us grow by identifying companies that could benefit from hiring through our platform, starting conversations with decision\-makers and building a pipeline of future customers. You do not need previous sales experience or a degree. We are looking for people who are curious, resilient, motivated by our mission and excited to learn. About Tangent Tangent is creating a fairer way for people to apply for jobs. We connect jobseekers with industry expert mentors who volunteer their time to support people with CVs, applications, interview preparation, industry advice and salary negotiation guidance. Our mentors work at leading companies and use their experience to help others navigate the hiring process with more confidence. Too often, access to top jobs depends on who you know, the advice you can afford and the networks you already have. Tangent exists to change that. As our network grows across London, Dublin, Berlin, Amsterdam, New York and beyond, we are building a team that can help more employers discover talented people from a wider range of backgrounds. We live by our product Our mission is so important to us that we have built it into our own hiring process. When you apply through Tangent, we want you to get a feel for what we are building and why it matters. That means giving candidates access to the wider Tangent platform, including our network of over 3,000 volunteer expert mentors. Mentors can support with interview preparation, industry advice, application guidance and salary negotiation advice. This support is optional and is designed to help candidates build confidence, understand the industry and make informed career decisions. Mentor support is separate from our hiring assessment and does not influence whether someone is selected for this role. What you’ll do As a Customer Success Associate, you will help employers succeed on Tangent and make sure they have a great experience using our platform. Your work will include: * Supporting customers\- Act as a day\-to\-day point of contact for employers using Tangent, helping them understand how to get the best results from the platform. * Onboarding new employers \- Guide new customers through the setup process, explain how Tangent works and help them launch successfully. * Building relationships \- Develop strong relationships with hiring teams, recruiters, people teams and decision\-makers. * Helping customers reach their goals\- Understand each customer’s hiring priorities and help them use Tangent to connect with relevant jobseekers. * Solving problems\- Respond to customer questions, troubleshoot issues and work with the team to improve the customer experience. * Driving engagement \- Encourage customers to stay active, post opportunities, review candidates and make the most of Tangent’s network. * Sharing feedback \- Bring customer insights back to the product, sales and marketing teams so we can keep improving Tangent. * Spotting growth opportunities \- Identify where customers could benefit from more support, additional roles or deeper partnership with Tangent. What we’re looking for We are looking for someone who is: * Passionate about Tangent’s mission * Empathetic and good at understanding people’s needs * Confident communicating with customers * Organised and able to manage multiple relationships * Curious and willing to learn * Comfortable solving problems and asking good questions * Interested in startups, customer success, recruitment, technology or social impact * Motivated by helping people and companies succeed You do not need to have worked in customer success before. You do not need a degree. We welcome applications from graduates, non\-graduates and career changers. Training and support This is an entry\-level role, so we will help you build the skills you need to succeed. You will learn how to: * Onboard new customers * Build strong customer relationships * Run effective customer check\-ins * Understand hiring needs * Support customers using a platform * Communicate customer feedback to internal teams * Use customer success tools and CRM systems * Identify opportunities to improve customer outcomes * Communicate Tangent’s mission and value clearly You will also be joining Tangent at an exciting stage of growth, with the opportunity to take on more responsibility as the company scales. What success looks like In your first few months, success will mean learning Tangent’s mission, understanding our customers and building confidence supporting employers on the platform. Over time, you will be expected to: * Help customers launch successfully on Tangent * Build trusted relationships with hiring teams * Increase customer engagement with the platform * Support customers in reaching their hiring goals * Share useful feedback that helps improve Tangent * Identify opportunities to grow customer accounts * Contribute ideas that improve the customer experience Why join Tangent? * Real social impact \- Your work will help more jobseekers access career opportunities with the support of expert mentors. * Career growth \- You will join a growing company with opportunities to progress as the team expands. * Learn sales from the ground up \- This is a chance to build valuable commercial skills in a supportive, mission\-led environment. * Share options \- Be rewarded with share options as Tangent grows. * A mission\-driven team \- Work with a passionate and ambitious team trying to make hiring fairer. Our mission Top jobs too often go to people with existing networks, privilege and access to specialist support. Tangent is helping to change that by connecting talented people from diverse backgrounds with expert mentors and forward\-thinking employers. If you are excited by our mission and want to help more people get hired, we would love to hear from you. Tangent is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, including those who may not have followed a traditional career path.

title: Customer Support Representative
description: **Description** **Ready to take your career global?** Make your mark at one of the biggest names in payments. We’re looking for a Customer Support Representative to join our everevolving customer suppose team and help shape the future of global commerce. **What You’ll Own** * Resolves client and/or merchant technical support issues relating to point of service (POS) products, including, but not limited to, dial\-up card swipe terminals, and provides standardized responses by utilizing established documentation and processes. Enters required data into databases while resolving customer issues and achieving established goals and objectives for quality and productivity measures. * Begins to build base knowledge of POS products, policies, customer service procedures, and an understanding of compliance and association guidelines through on\-going training and referring to online manuals as required. **What You’ll Bring** * Skills / Knowledge \- Acquires and applies job skills and learns company policies and procedures to complete assigned routine tasks. * Job Complexity \- Works on assignments that are routine to semi\-routine in nature, requiring limited decision outside of stated processes, but recognizes the need for occasional deviation from accepted practice. Has little or no role in the decision\-making. * Supervision \- Normally receives detailed instructions and follows established procedures on all work, requires instructions on all assignments. Works under close supervision. * Computer Skills \- Basic computer skills * Industry Knowledge \- Ability to develop basic payments industry knowledge * Research \- Basic ability to research issues and resolve customer inquiries * High School Diploma or Equivalent * Typically No Relevant Experience Required **It’s a bonus if you have** * Think like a client: We care deeply about our clients' success. We ask, listen and learn — curious to understand first. Our passion drives excellence in everything we set out to do. * Act like an owner: We take accountability — working at pace to own our outcomes. We empower each other to make decisions. We’re innovative — always finding better ways to deliver impact. * Win as one team: We value personal connections — moving forward together with clarity. We build inclusive and global teams — embracing different perspectives and ideas. It’s always ‘we,’ never ‘me.’ We’re determined to succeed together. **About The Team** Our inclusive and global teams win together every day. We’re proud to have the best minds in the industry, who you can learn from as you grow your career. The people, the energy, the connections – it’s unmatched. Come and be part of an ever\-evolving company and get dynamic opportunities that go beyond borders. **What makes a Globalpayer?** Globalpayers think like a client, act like an owner and win as one team. We’re curious and innovative – always finding better ways to deliver impact. We empower each other to make decisions, and it’s our passion that drives excellence in everything we set out to do. Does this sound like you? Then you sound like a Globalpayer. Apply now to take your career global.

title: Cyber Security Engineer
description: **Cybersecurity Engineer \- Daily Rate up to €600** * Long term, rolling contract * Daily rate up to €600 * Flexible, hybrid work setting One of the world's leading manufacturers of food processing solutions is enhancing compliance with the Cyber Resilience Act. As an **Cybersecurity Engineer** , you will play a critical role in ensuring compliance with relevant regulations and legislation while working with Embedded Linux systems and Azure IoT Edge devices. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who is hungry for a new challenge and eager to join a supportive, collaborative team of experts. **Why You'll Love It Here:** * Daily rate €550\-€600 * Hybrid work setting offering flexibility * Opportunity to influence cybersecurity strategy and compliance * Collaborative international engineering environment * Cutting\-edge edge computing and connected device technologies * Long\-term contract opportunity with strong extension potential **You Will:** * Take ownership of Linux\-based operating systems * Lead the evolution of automated build and release processes, helping deliver secure IoT solutions faster * Implement security controls including secure boot, encryption and certificate management, showing immediate impact **Skills / Experience:** * Experience with Linux * Cyber Security Experience * Azure IoT For more information, contact nadja.voglar@archer.ie For additional opportunities, visit the Archer Recruitment website

title: Data Analyst
description: **Strong SQL Skills Required** The Data Analyst will report to the Director Data Intelligence and will use internal and external data to provide actionable insights. This individual will work closely with data scientists, data engineers, software developers and SME’s within the business to ensure the delivery of accurate analytics and reporting. The Data Analyst will derive understanding and insights via detailed analytics that will lead to increased operational efficiency and effectiveness by enabling proactive data\-driven decisions across the organization. **Key Responsibilities:** * Dissect and solve fleet related business problems using analytical and quantitative approaches to identify patterns and correlations in large, complex datasets. * You will provide data backed solutions to questions such as the below: * What is the optimal length of time to hold a vehicle? * How can we reduce vehicle out of service time? * What is the driving factor behind increased vehicle damage rates? * Provide actionable insight to decision\-makers to influence strategy and performance. * Integrate and curate data from a wide range of internal and external sources to develop detailed reports. * Use data visualization tools to explain your results simply and succinctly to technical, non\-technical, and senior audiences. **Required Education** A bachelor's degree or higher in a quantitative discipline such as statistics, mathematics, physics, engineering or computer science. **Required Skills and Experience** * Minimum of 3 years hands\-on experience in a data analyst role. * Minimum of 2 years hands on experience with Databricks (or equivalent). * Strong capabilities in SQL and experience working with structured and unstructured data. * Strong problem solving and critical thinking skills with a proven record for identifying and diagnosing problems, and solving complex problems with simple, logical solutions. * Strong experience with Data Visualization tools such as Tableau * Outstanding verbal, written, presentation, facilitation, and interaction skills, including * \[Nice to Have] Python scripting skills. ***Hertz is an equal opportunity employer, all applicants must have the legal right to work in Ireland (Valid EU Passport / Relevant V* isa).**

title: Data Analyst - Housing Management
description: **Job Description: Data Analyst \- Housing Management** **Responsible To: Head of Housing** **Responsible For: N/A** **Location: Dublin with hybrid working** **Contract: Full Time Permanent** Job Summary: As Housing Data Analyst your role is to support the Housing Department by collecting, managing, and analysing data across its core functions. You will deliver accurate, timely, and insightful reporting and dashboards to inform strategic decision\-making, service improvement, and operational planning. This role will provide data analytics and reporting across Housing, Lettings, RTB, ASB, Tenant Engagement, and Tenant Sustainability. The following list is typical of the level of duties which the post holder is expected to perform or be responsible for. It is not necessarily exhaustive and other duties of a similar type and level may be expected from time to time. The post holder will be responsible for a combination of the objectives described below and annual KPIs depending on the requirements of the organisation and the priorities of the Housing Department. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES (Functional): Data Collection, Validation and Preparation * Gather data from multiple sources (databases, spreadsheets, APIs) ensuring accuracy, completeness, and consistency. * Clean, filter, and transform raw data, identifying and removing inconsistencies, errors, and outliers. * Conduct data validation checks and document data definitions, business rules, and assumptions. Data Analysis and Insight Generation * Analyse large data sets to identify trends, patterns, and areas of risk or opportunity within property services. * Apply statistical techniques to generate actionable insights across key areas. * Support decision\-making by interpreting and presenting insights clearly to internal stakeholders. Reporting and Dashboard Development * Design, build, and maintain regular and ad\-hoc reports and dashboards using tools such as Power BI. * Tailor reporting outputs for different internal audiences across sub\-teams. * Ensure accuracy, timeliness, and clarity in all reporting deliverables. Business Intelligence and Continuous Improvement * Stay current with BI tools, automation techniques, and data analytics trends. * Identify and recommend improvements in existing reporting frameworks and data collection methods. * Support automation and self\-service capabilities for reporting users. Governance, Compliance and Audit * Ensure all data handling and analysis activities adhere to Tuath's data governance, privacy, and security policies. * Maintain clear documentation of data sources, methodologies, and reporting templates to support audit and regulatory requirements. * Monitor and analyse data to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory frameworks. Collaboration and Stakeholder Engagement * Work closely with internal departments (e.g. Property, Compliance, Finance) to understand data requirements and provide support. * Liaise with consultants, contractors, and external stakeholders or data collectors where relevant. * Provide clear reporting to senior management and participate in inter\-departmental meetings and briefings. Training, Support and Capacity Building * Provide training and support to end\-users on interpreting reports and using dashboards. * Promote a culture of data literacy and evidence\-based decision\-making across the organisation. Documentation and Communication * Document methodologies, assumptions, and limitations for all analyses conducted. * Prepare written reports (including audits, surveys, risk assessments) with recommendations and innovative solutions. * Communicate complex data findings in a clear, concise, and visual format for both technical and non\-technical audiences. KEY RESPONSIBIITIES (Organisational): * Provide input into the Operational plans. * Adopt a collaborative and supportive approach, maintaining up to date professional knowledge and providing advice and assistance to colleagues as required. * Provide statistical data, information, and testimonials for annual report. * In all aspects of the company's work, promote effective communications, excellence in customer service, and a focus on continuous improvement. * Be pro\-active in the development and maintaining of strong networks and relationships with colleagues and in other organisations and agencies, to ensure excellence in service delivery. Health and Safety Responsibility * To conduct all activities in a manner which is safe to yourself and others. To be aware and to act in accordance with Tuath's Health and Safety Policy. * Ensure all appropriate health and safety risk assessments are in place and making sure colleagues are aware of and adhere to any specific instructions and use personal protective equipment where required. Ensure that team members are aware of any risks associated with their role, adhere to any specific instructions, and use personal protective equipment where required. This job description is not definitive or restrictive and will be subject to periodic review.

title: Data Quality Analyst
description: **About us:** Amach is an industry\-leading technology driven company with headquarters located in Dublin and remote teams in UK and Europe. Our blended teams of local and nearshore talent are optimised to deliver high quality and collaborative solutions. Founded in 2013, Amach was created to solve a specific problem in aviation: too much complexity, too little usable intelligence. We help airlines modernise their operating model using cloud, data and Al\-delivered by teams with deep aviation domain expertise. Our goal is to maximize airlines' operational efficiency by optimizing resource use, reduce costs and increase customer experience and satisfaction. We are seeking a Data Quality Analyst to join a growing Data \& AI Governance team. This is a hands\-on role focused on improving the quality, trust, and accountability of data across the organisation. You will work closely with business stakeholders, data owners, and technology teams to identify data quality issues, perform root cause analysis, support remediation activities, and help drive the adoption of data governance practices. **Location:** Hybrid (2 days per week in Dublin office) **Key responsibilities \& duties include:** * Investigate and resolve data quality issues. * Define and monitor data quality rules and KPIs. * Gather and document business requirements. * Support the adoption of data governance processes and data catalogues. * Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders to improve data quality. * Produce documentation, reporting, and analysis to support governance initiatives. **Required Experience:** * 3\+ years' experience in Data Quality, Data Governance, Data Analysis, or a related field. * Strong SQL and data analysis skills. * Experience working with data quality management and governance programmes. * Strong stakeholder management and communication skills. * Experience creating business and technical documentation. **Desirable:** * Experience with data quality and observability tools such as Monte Carlo, Soda, or Informatica. * Experience with data catalogues such as Atlan, Collibra, or Purview. * Experience with Power BI, Snowflake, Databricks, Jira, and Confluence. **What’s in it for you:** * An opportunity to join a fast\-growing company * Options for career advancement * Learning and development opportunities * Flexible working environment * Competitive salaries based on experience **Equal Opportunity Employer:** Amach is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. This job description is intended to convey essential responsibilities and qualifications for this role, but it is not an exhaustive list of tasks that an employee may be required to perform. If you are passionate about driving customer success, advising on strategic solutions, and contributing to product innovation, we would love to hear from you! **Not for you?** Check out all of our open positions in our **careers page** and follow us on **LinkedIn** for future opportunities. P.S. Share this with friends and co\-workers! Don't be afraid they'll steal it from you, if you're amazing and smart we'll find a role for you. We are growing fast and we are always looking for talented people. At Amach, we strive to be an inclusive community of open\-minded individuals with different backgrounds and we are committed to fostering, cultivating and preserving a culture of diversity, equity and inclusion. We strongly believe that a diversity of experience and background is essential to create a fulfilling environment and better solutions for our people and our customers. All Amach employees and contractors are expected to honour this policy and act to ensure that every individual is respected in the workplace. **Your personal data** Amach will process your personal information in accordance with the EU's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). We will comply with data protection law and principles, which means that your data will be: * Used lawfully, fairly and in a transparent way * Collected only for valid purposes and not used in any way that is incompatible with those purposes * Relevant to the purposes we have told you about and limited only to those purposes * Accurate and kept up to date * Kept only as long as necessary for the purposes we have told you about * Kept securely If you would like to contact us about your data, please use the following address: info@amach.com

title: DevOps Engineer (€60k)
description: Some companies use DevOps to ship apps faster. This team is using it to help reshape how future medicines are discovered. We’re working with a fast\-growing deep\-tech company operating at the intersection of AI, computational science, and pharmaceutical innovation. Their platform is tackling one of the biggest challenges in drug development,, using advanced simulation and crystal structure prediction technology to accelerate research that traditionally takes years. Now they’re looking for a DevOps Engineer who loves building systems from the ground up. Not just maintaining infrastructure… but architecting the engine that powers cutting\-edge scientific computing. **This is the kind of role where you’ll:** ⚙️ Build self\-hosted CI/CD systems engineers actually enjoy using 📦 Orchestrate containerised workloads across distributed infrastructure 📊 Design observability stacks that give real\-time visibility into complex systems 🛠 Own infrastructure\-as\-code and automation from day one 🧠 Work directly with highly technical R\&D teams and leadership on scaling architecture 🚀 Help create an environment where experimentation and innovation can move faster, safely They’re not looking for someone who waits for tickets to appear. They want someone curious, hands\-on, and excited by solving hard infrastructure problems in a highly technical environment. **What you’ll likely bring:** • Strong experience in DevOps / Platform Engineering / SRE • Confidence working with Docker, Podman, or container ecosystems • Experience building CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or similar • Hands\-on knowledge of observability and monitoring stacks (Prometheus, Grafana, alerting systems, etc.) • Infrastructure\-as\-Code experience with tools like Ansible • Strong Linux fundamentals and scripting skills • Python proficiency for automation and tooling • A self\-starter mindset with the ability to work closely with technical and non\-technical stakeholders alike **Why this role stands out:** This isn’t another “keep the lights on” DevOps role. You’ll be joining a highly innovative, award\-winning environment where infrastructure directly supports scientific breakthroughs, AI\-driven research, and next\-generation pharmaceutical technology. If you enjoy autonomy, technical depth, and building scalable systems that actually matter, this is worth a conversation.

title: Electrical Engineer, Data Center
description: Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: **Dublin, Ireland; Amsterdam, Netherlands; London, UK** . **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering or related technical field or equivalent practical experience. * 10 years of experience in hyperscale and cololocation data center design and execution. **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience in the detailed design of substations and substation designs between 110kV to 400kV. * Experience managing vendor relationships, with an understanding of standard practices in the design and construction industry. * Experience working with data center equipment/environments (including switchgear, generators, controls, security monitoring systems, fire safety systems), with an understanding of start up/commissioning processes. * Understanding of the various data center electrical topologies. * Expertise with CAD/ACC/BIM software packages. * Ability to travel 15% of the time as needed. **About The Job** Our thirst for technology is a part of everything we do. The Data Center Engineering team takes the physical design of our data centers into the future. Our lab mirrors a research and development department \-\- cutting\-edge strategies are born, tested and tested again. Along with a team of great minds, you take on complex topics like how we use power or how to run state\-of\-the\-art, environmentally\-friendly facilities. You're a visionary who optimizes for efficiencies and never stops seeking improvements \-\- even small changes that can make a huge impact. You generate ideas, communicate recommendations to senior\-level executives and drive implementation alongside facilities technicians. In this role, you will be responsible for the development and delivery of large\-scale systems and infrastructure from site assessments and concept design to greenfield construction, along with modification of existing infrastructures on global capital projects. You will provide and prepare all types of documents including: complex analytical assessments, across multiple layers of large\-scale data, critical risk assessment input and critical de\-risking recommendations at all business levels, summaries and presentations to facilitate LT decision making, Scope of Work (SOW), concept designs, design specifications, calculations, detailed integrated design deliverables, drawing markup, milestone reviews, budget, schedule, system test and commissioning, acceptance documents, and review and acceptance of as\-builts and submittals. Behind everything our users see online is the architecture built by the Technical Infrastructure team to keep it running. From developing and maintaining our data centers to building the next generation of Google platforms, we make Google's product portfolio possible. We're proud to be our engineers' engineers and love voiding warranties by taking things apart so we can rebuild them. We keep our networks up and running, ensuring our users have the best and fastest experience possible. Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €138000 \- €142000 (EUR) \+ 20% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Netherlands: €144000 \- €148000 (EUR) \+ 20% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits **Responsibilities** Learn more about benefits at Google . * Drive project electrical outcomes of high complexity and scope as a key contributor, integrating feedback from multiple stakeholders, when necessary. * Design and localize new/upgrades and expansions of hyperscale data centers and campuses. * Update/maintain internal design specifications, drawings and standards to the latest configurations. * Work with cross\-functional teams in construction, design and change management to compile, prioritize/triage and process construction originated engineering issues for standard design/product improvements. * Review and assist technical development of electrical systems (HV,MV, LV) for hyperscale data centers including campus planning, general layout/arrangements, PUE calculations, life cycle carbon assessments. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Environmental Health Safety Advisor
description: Location: Kildare, Leixlip Employment Type: Contract Date Posted: 17/Jun/2026 Arcadis works at the frontier of facility delivering innovative and ground\-breaking solutions for our clients on the most exciting and high\-profile projects around the world, designing some of the most complex and specialized facilities under construction today. Our European business is growing, and we are recruiting key team members. Look no further if you would like to work on one of the biggest and leading\-edge construction projects in the world and be part of a winning team. People with skills, knowledge, and expertise like yours underpin our success. A career at Arcadis is truly rewarding, with excellent benefits and opportunities to develop your technical and professional potential. We respect and support each other to create one Arcadis team. We are dynamic, we create space for ideas, independent views, and challenging the status quo. We have a diverse workforce and embrace different views and perspectives. Look no further if you would like to be part of a vibrant dynamic team that delivers world\-class engineering, design, and project management for industry\-leading clients around the world. If you have what it takes to work with us, apply today. **Role Summary** **Industrial Hygiene Scope** Co\-ordination, continuous improvement and management of specific Industrial Hygiene (IH) programs onsite which may also involve close liaison with Safety/Industrial Hygiene engineers. Examples of site IH programs include hearing conservation, ionising and non\-ionising radiation, ppe and ventilation. In addition you will support the overall EHS management system and OHSAS 18001 / ISO 45001 standards which will involve close liaison with EHS engineers. Your responsibilities will include but not be limited to: * Conducting IH risk assessments and associated employee and/or area exposure monitoring for both physical and chemical hazards. * Supporting IH surveys associated with our Equipment Sign Off (ESO) program. * Act as the content expert in relation to assigned IH programs. * Support accident/incident investigation and root cause analysis. * Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance to regulatory requirements and internal standards. * Analyze data, identify trends and define focus areas. **Environmental Scope:** Responsible for the development, co\-ordination, continuous improvement and management of site environmental programmes, in particular wastewater, and potentially support other programmes such as air emissions, noise, refrigerants, groundwater and management systems. Your responsibilities will include but not be limited to: * Playing a key role in ensuring compliance with the site’s Industrial Emissions Licence and ISO14001 standard. This includes review of compliance results/trends, Env Mgt System elements including awareness/training, documentation, Environmental Management Programme projects etc., incident investigation and root cause analysis. * Understand how all environmental programmes work by supporting all Environmental Engineers at the site with a view to becoming a primary owner for some environmental programme(s), with additional responsibility as secondary owner for other programmes. **Safety Scope:** Co\-ordination, continuous improvement and management of specific safety programs onsite which may also involve close liaison with Safety engineers. Examples of site safety programs include Powered Industrial Trucks, Working at Heights etc. In addition you will support the overall EHS management system and OHSAS 18001 / ISO 45001 standards which will involve close liaison with EHS engineers. Your responsibilities will include but not be limited to: * Conducting safety risk assessments * Act as the content expert in relation to assigned safety programs. * Support accident/incident investigation and root cause analysis. * Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance to regulatory requirements and internal standards. * Analyze data, identify trends and define focus areas. **Minimum Qualifications:** A minimum of a NFQ Level 7 qualification related to the role (e.g. environmental sciences, sustainability, chemical engineering, environmental health \& safety, sciences) or have completed either a Post. Grad. Diploma or MSc. related to the role. Level 8 qualification is preferable. **Preferred requirements** * 3\+ years’ work experience related to the role is beneficial. * Ability to work in a team with minimum direction. Self\-motivator with own initiative. * Demonstrate excellent communication skills, both verbal and written and the ability to present and explain environmental information to a broad audience of both technical and non\-technical people. * Excellent English \- written and verbal * Knowledge of some of the EHS areas mentioned above. * A high tolerance for ambiguity and change due to the dynamic nature of the business. * Demonstrate efficient work methods, analytical \& problem\-solving skills and ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast\-paced environment. * Demonstrate excellent attention to detail * Willingness to build and foster a strong EHS culture

Candidates should be physically fit, willing to work outdoors in all weather conditions, and able to work effectively as part of a team. **What We Offer:** * Daily rate: **€150 per day** * Monday to Friday: **7:30am – 5:00pm** * Optional weekend overtime available * Steady, long\-term work * Training and career progression opportunities for the right candidate **Requirements:** * Previous construction or fencing experience is an advantage but not essential * Strong work ethic and reliability * Safe Pass and Manual Handling certification preferred * Full driving licence beneficial If you are motivated, dependable, and looking for a hands\-on role with a growing company, we would like to hear from you.
