title: Field Service Engineer
description: **About Ekco** 🚀 Founded in 2016 Ekco is now one of the fastest growing cloud solution providers in Europe! We specialise in enabling companies to progress along the path of cloud maturity, managing transformation and driving better outcomes from our clients’ existing technology investments. ☁️ In a few words, we take businesses to the cloud and back! 🌍 We have over 1000 highly talented and supportive colleagues (and counting) across a number of regional offices in the UK, Ireland, Benelux, South Africa \& Malaysia. **The role** You will be responsible for providing on\-site technical support on site in Dublin including troubleshooting, diagnosing and resolving second\-line issues in a timely manner. You will manage tickets within your queue and ensure clear, professional communication with clients, 3rd\-party vendors, team members, team leads, managers and other departments as required. The role requires a proactive approach to learning new technologies, processes and tools, as well as the ability to deliver excellent customer service while working both independently and as part of a team. **Key Responsibilities** * Receive and respond to escalated customer calls, emails, tickets, and alerts through ConnectWise. * Work independently ensuring all tickets, alerts, escalations, documentation, and issue handling are completed accurately and proactively. * Troubleshoot advanced issues across Microsoft 365, AVD/RDS, AD/Entra ID, Exchange Online, Intune, Group Policy, networking basics, and VMware. * Implement approved changes following Change Management processes. * Perform trend analysis and raise/manage Problem tickets for recurring issues. * Create or update scripts (e.g., PowerShell) to automate tasks and improve efficiency. * Monitor system alerts and RMM dashboards, responding quickly and proactively. * Carry out scheduled maintenance tasks including patching, updates, reboot cycles, and validation checks. * Communicate clearly with customers, ensuring accurate updates and a strong customer experience. * Manage personal and shared queues to ensure escalated tickets progress and resolve within SLA. * Collaborate with 3rd Line, Cloud, Networking, and Security teams to support escalations and resolutions. * Produce and maintain high‑quality documentation and knowledge base articles. * Support, mentor, and guide L1 engineers to build team capability * Identify improvement opportunities and contribute to continual service improvement. **Education** **Key Requirements** * College Degree (Three\-year college or technical school) Preferred, Field of Study: IT or related technical field is an advantage. **Experience** * Minimum of 2\-4 years’ experience as a service desk engineer. Proven knowledge of a ticketing system and RMM system. Experience working with Office 365, Azure and Active Directory. Proven experience with Windows and Mac desktop and server operating systems. Experience working with networks and networking hardware. **IT Skills** * Knowledge of Windows and Mac OS –Configuration and troubleshooting * Knowledge troubleshooting mobile devices for mail configuration and MFA setup. * Basic administration of Office 365, Azure, Active Directory and MS Office. * Knowledge of networking hardware: switches, wireless access points, network firewalls and routers. * Knowledge of peripherals used by our clients. Docks, mice, keyboards, monitors etc. * Knowledge of networking principles, administration of firewalls and networking including VPN. * Administration of Windows Server OS’ and virtualization * Testing and supporting technology such as sensor gateways in client homes. * Experience in ICT support. * Hypervisor and SAN knowledge is an advantage **Certifications** IT industry certifications are preferred, specifically CompTia A\+ and Network \+. Microsoft 355 and Azure certifications also. Driver’s License: Required if residing in Ireland. **Benefits/Perks** * ☀️ Time off \- 25 days leave \+ public holidays * 🎂 x1 day Birthday leave per year * 💰 Company Pension Scheme (employer contribution) \+ flexible salary sacrifice * 📞 Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) \- access to dedicated mental health, emotional wellbeing and general advice * 🏃‍♀️ EkcOlympics \- a global activity for fun! * 📚 Learning \& development \- Unlimited access to Pluralsight learning platform * 🌱 A lot of responsibilities \& opportunities to grow (also internationally) **Why Ekco** * ⭐️ Microsoft’s 2023 Rising Star Security Partner of the year * 🚀 VMware \& Veeam top partner status * 🏅 Ranked as 4th fastest growing technology company in the Deloitte Fast50 Awards * 🌈Ekco are committed to cultivating an environment that promotes diversity, equality, inclusion and belonging * 🎉 We recognise the value of internal mobility and encourage opportunities for internal development \& progression * ✨ Flexible working with a family friendly focus are at the core of our company values

title: Finance Business Partner
description: My client, one of Ireland’s leading architectural firms, and based in Central Dublin, are seeking a commercially focused Finance Business Partner to support a group of Directors, senior architects, and project leads. The role will own financial performance across a portfolio of projects, providing strategic insight, driving profitability, strengthening controls, and supporting commercial decision\-making. This is an opportunity for an experienced finance professional who thrives in a project\-based environment and can operate independently while influencing senior stakeholders. **Key Responsibilities** * Act as the lead finance partner for a portfolio of projects and business units. * Own project financial performance, including revenue, profitability, cash flow, forecasting, and resource planning. * Provide commercial insight and challenge to support business decisions, pricing, and growth opportunities. * Monitor project budgets, margins, WIP, and billing performance, identifying risks and recommending corrective actions. * Deliver financial analysis, forecasting, and reporting to support strategic planning and operational performance. * Partner with Directors and project teams to improve commercial outcomes and financial discipline. * Review commercial terms, contracts, and proposals to ensure profitability and risk management. * Drive continuous improvement in financial processes, reporting, controls, and the adoption of AI\-enabled solutions. * Support and mentor junior finance team members where required. **Candidate Profile** * Qualified Accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent). * 5\+ years' post\-qualified experience in Finance Business Partnering, Commercial Finance, or Project Finance. * Strong experience in budgeting, forecasting, financial modelling, and performance analysis. * Knowledge of project accounting, revenue recognition, WIP, and margin management. * Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and translate financial data into commercial actions. * Advanced Excel skills; Power BI experience desirable. * Experience within architecture, engineering, construction, property, consultancy, or other project\-based professional services environments is advantageous. * Commercially astute and highly analytical. * Self\-directed, proactive, and comfortable operating with autonomy. * Strong communicator with the ability to influence non\-finance stakeholders. * Able to balance strategic thinking with attention to detail. * Results\-oriented with a continuous improvement mindset. **Salary and Benefits** * Salary\- €80,000\-€90,000 * Bonus * Bonus voucher paid to all employees yearly * Hybrid working * Pension – matched pension contribution up to 3% * Annual Leave – 21 days plus Good Friday * Learning \& Development – financial support for relevant courses (fees paid and paid study leave), in\-house training For more information on this role or any other Accountancy roles contact Conal O'Connor or e\-mail or click on the apply button below To view all live jobs with Brightwater and market insights, please visit our website; www.brightwater.ie

title: Financial Accountant
description: **We’re reinventing payments.** In less than four years, Dojo disrupted the market to become the largest and most loved acquirer in the UK. Our payments infrastructure, purpose\-built for in\-person commerce, is game changing. Now, over 150,000 customers across four countries choose to transact billions with us every year. But we’re just getting started. Our people are the driving force behind our success. They are our greatest investment and our ultimate competitive advantage. We hire exceptional people and give them the autonomy, trust, and ownership to thrive. The results take care of themselves. ****The Role**** Dojo is expanding rapidly. We’ve built a leading presence in the UK, and we are now focused on bringing our experience to the rest of Europe. We are looking for a strategic partner for our UK parent company — someone to provide the financial foundation for our Irish entity as we scale into Spain, Italy, and France. Reporting directly to our **Head of Finance (PCF 11\)** , you will be responsible for our regulatory integrity. You will ensure we meet our obligations to the Central Bank of Ireland with precision and transparency, providing the stable financial governance needed to support our international growth. This role is ideal for someone who values technical excellence in safeguarding. ****What you will do…**** * Lead the preparation and submission of financial and statistical regulatory returns to the CBI, ensuring our reporting is consistently accurate and timely. * Manage the daily safeguarding reconciliation process, maintaining full compliance with CBI Safeguarding Guidance. * Take responsibility for the Safeguarding Framework, ensuring our policies and procedures are robust and ready to support a multi\-region business. * Monitor the firm’s regulatory capital and liquidity positions to ensure we are always operating safely within E\-Money Regulations. * As we launch \& scale across Europe, you will provide the technical accounting and regulatory insights required to support our entry into those territories. * Drive the monthly financial close and audit processes while partnering with the UK Group Treasury and Finance teams on initiatives ****What you will bring...**** * ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or CPA qualified * Proven industry experience, ideally within a FinTech, Payments, or regulated financial services environment * Strong working knowledge of CBI reporting and Safeguarding requirements. * A data\-driven approach to problem\-solving and a natural curiosity for how systems and flows work. * You can translate complex regulatory data into clear, actionable insights for the PCF 11 and other senior stakeholders. * Naturally curious about how systems work and are always looking for ways to improve processes. **Dojo home and away** We believe our best work happens when we collaborate in\-person. These “together days” foster communication, drive innovation and spark our brightest ideas. That's why we have an office\-first culture. This means working from the office 4\+ days per week. With offices across Europe, we know a thing or two about staying dynamic. Need deep focus? Head to a quiet zone. Big ideas? Collaboration spaces have you covered. Just here for a catch\-up? Our social hubs make it easy. Do work that counts, in spaces made for you. **Question: what’s curious, relentless, and customer obsessed?** If you’re keen to know the answer, you’re a third of the way to meeting our Dojo values. **If The Following Speak To You, Let’s Talk** * You’re curious. You have a real desire to learn and create. * You’re relentless. You keep going even when it’s easier not to. * You’re customer\-obsessed. You know how important customers are to what you do. **Diversity, equity, and inclusion at Dojo** From local bakeries to well\-known eateries, Dojo payments serve over 150,000 places across the UK. And something that’s fundamental to creating relevant, innovative products at Dojo is to build teams to reflect the diversity of the businesses we serve. Our drive to improve diversity, equity, and inclusion is closely linked to helping employees thrive and innovating for better customer experiences. If you care about your work, you’re curious, and you think customer\-first, you have a place at Dojo. To make sure you’re the best you can be throughout the recruitment process, let us know if you need any extra adjustments to help you thrive. Visit dojo.careers to find out more about our benefits and what it’s like to work at Dojo, or check out our LinkedIn and Instagram pages.

title: Financial Advisor
description: Sherry FitzGerald Financial Services (SFFS) is a growing division of the Sherry FitzGerald Group incorporating our Mortgages and Life \& Pensions businesses. With over 25 years’ experience in the property sector, we give our customers expert financial advice at every stage of the property process, from mortgages \& insurance to pensions \& investments. As part of its ongoing growth strategy SFFS are looking to recruit a number of Financial Advisors. The successful candidate(s) will become part of an expanding team that provides financial advice across mortgages, life, pensions and investments. Leveraging new technologies, a strong corporate brand and relationships with Ireland’s leading lenders and life \& pensions providers, the financial advisor team have a firm focus on supporting the Group’s growth strategy and targets. **Role \& Responsibilities** * Support the financial needs of SFFS clients through the provision of advice across mortgages, life, insurances, pensions, investments and savings. * Recommend and provide tailored solutions to match SFFS client requirements ensuring adherence to all regulatory and compliance standards. * Optimise all internal and external business opportunities to deliver income impact to the SFFS P\&L. * Identify \& act on referrals as they present themselves across Sherry FitzGerald Group (e.g. Residential, New Homes divisions etc). * Achieve KPI targets including annual income, applications, conversion, customer service and profitability metrics. * Proactively develop new business opportunities through relationships, networking and business development activities to enhance the SFFS brand and reputation in the community. * Provide and maintain excellent customer service to clients at all stages of their journey with SFFS. * Be an effective member of the SFFS team contributing to the delivery of the annual business plan, while supporting each other in the achievement of KPIs. * Use initiative to identify business opportunities and make recommendations that should be pursued to grow revenue, market share and improve service levels for SFFS. * Manage workload to exceed our customers’ expectations by producing work to the highest professional standard, representing the brand in the best way possible. * Live the Company Values in all dealings with colleagues and take responsibility for own development by undertaking activities to broaden experience and market knowledge, while also sharing expertise and providing coaching to others. **Requirements** * 5\+ years relevant experience in the Financial Advice industry * Qualified to QFA or similar industry level * Experience of mortgage advice \& sales with track record of achieving annual targets * Experience of mortgage packaging \& underwriting processes * Experience of the Life \& Pensions market and products would be an advantage * Knowledge of the Irish Property Market * Excellent interpersonal \& communication skills, good ability to multitask and meet deadlines * Highly motivated with an ability to take ownership and strong attention to detail * Experience of working in/with the broker industry would be advantageous * Comfortable with new technologies and proficient in MS office packages * Full clean drivers licence and access to your own vehicle Sherry FitzGerald is an equal opportunity employer. All job applicants are considered solely on their ability to meet the requirements of the role, and selection criteria will reflect the needs of the position.

title: Financial Analyst Group Reporting - Dublin
description: **Country:** Ireland **Req ID:** 524697 **Job Type** : Full Time **Workplace Type** : Hybrid **Seniority Level** : Entry Level **About CRH** CRH is the leading provider of building materials critical to modernizing infrastructure. with out team of 83,000 people across 4,000 locations, our unmatched scale, connected portfolio, and deep local relationships make us the partner of choice for transportation, water, and reindustrialization projects, shaping communities for a better tomorrow. CRH (NYSE: CRH) is a member of the S\&P 500 Index. For more information, visit www.crh.com. Without you noticing our products, we are everywhere you live, work, and relax. Our project portfolio includes some of the most sustainable and cutting\-edge building projects around the world. Think of the asphalt on the Silverstone Grand Prix Circuit, the Paris Metro Rail project, but also the Louis Vuitton Museum in Paris, parts of the Burj Khalifa, and the Kennedy Space Centre. The R\&PA team forms part of the CRH Group Finance function, is a high performing team and a valued business partner that leads a number of key internal and external reporting processes. This includes monthly Board and management reporting, financial planning and analysis, forecast and annual Budget; the team also prepares quarterly briefing materials to support external results announcements and business performance related sections of the 10\-Q/10\-K. The wider R\&PA team is divided up into two sub teams, Reporting and FP\&A, each of which has ownership for specific processes and deliverables and is the central liaison for the Divisions. **This role is a financial analyst position on the R\&PA Reporting sub\-team.** The Reporting team is responsible for: * Monthly Board and management reporting including review and analysis of their key performance metrics, providing detailed analysis and commentary to Board and senior management of drivers of performance; * Annual Budget reporting process including review and analysis of their key performance metrics, providing the related output reporting and analysis for Group preliminary and final budget to the Board and executive team; * Governance and reporting of Capital Expenditure for the Divisions, including tranche allocation and detailed analysis of major capital projects on a quarterly basis; * Building a collaborative and functional relationship with CRH colleagues in the Divisional Finance teams, identifying solutions and resolving complex issues as they arise; * Review of management to financial results reconciliation for the Division at quarter\-ends; * Provision of high\-quality and timely ad\-hoc analysis to senior management and business partners. Hybrid working at present, with the team ordinarily based at Group HQ in Stonemasons Way, Dublin. **Key Tasks And Responsibilities** * Provide critical analysis for the monthly Board and management reporting processes including review of the results submitted by the Divisions and ownership of the related outputs with a key focus on critical trend analysis, key performance metrics and drivers of performance. * Ownership and review of the preliminary and final budget outputs for the Group including Board and management presentations, reports and analysis. * Review and analysis of financial reporting notes and disclosures, as part of the Group financial consolidation process. * Ability to identify and resolve reporting issues that arise, identifying solutions while working closely with Divisional Finance and other teams across Group Finance. * Assistance in system developments that are required in Hyperion Financial Management (HFM) for reporting purposes, liaising with the Hyperion support team. * Support the implementation of EPM for Reporting, actively involve in UAT, parallel run activities and Go‑Live readiness review, while help establishing aligned Management Reporting Guidelines to support a consistent, future‑state reporting framework. * Focus on continuous improvements in all processes and outputs to add value and drive business results. * Build strong relationships while working extensively with Divisional Finance teams on new reporting requirements and analysis in order to provide greater insight into their performance and results. * Provide high quality ad hoc analysis to senior management as requested to support decision making. **Key Competencies** * Team player with ability to contribute both individually and as part of a team. * Demonstrate integrity and trust, with ability to maintain discretion and confidentiality. * Self\-motivated with a high degree of initiative; comfortable working in a demanding fast\-paced environment. * Strong communication skills and comfortable liaising with senior management. * Good financial and analytical skills, with excellent attention to detail and a focus on results. * Ability to multitask within tight deadlines. **Education And Experience** * Qualified accountant with up to 2 years PQE * Strong academic background, professional record and qualification (ACA, CFA or equivalent) * Experience working on high performing teams, ideally across different geographies * Good systems capabilities and/or interest in financial reporting systems; experience with HFM system would be an advantage * Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint and Word * Exposure to Group reporting and processes. The nature of the role is outlined above. Flexibility is required within the role, which includes some core elements but also periodic emphasis on more project\-type activities. **What CRH Offers You** * A culture that values opportunity for growth, development, and internal promotion * Highly competitive salary package * Comprehensive secondary benefits * Significant contribution to your pension plan * Health and wellness programs, including an on\-site gym and fitness classes * Excellent opportunities to develop and progress with a global organization **Connect your future to CRH** We are curious to learn more about you. At CRH, we believe our mutual differences contribute to the healthy, productive, and enjoyable workspace we create. Please introduce yourself and send us your application. Following a positive review of your application, you will be invited to an introductory call with one of our Recruiters. Is this role not for you, but do you know someone who would love to join the team? Please let us know! CRH finds it important that vacancies are shared to individuals that may find them interesting and/or could be suitable for the role Please contact our recruitment team at careers@crh.com. *CRH is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to creating an inclusive work environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.* *Benefits/perks listed above may vary depending on the nature of the employment with CRH and the country where you work.* ***Please note that we cannot accept any applications submitted through email for GDPR purposes. Candidates must apply through our job portal.*** ***We do not accept candidate introductions for this position from recruitment agencies, unless you have been instructed to do so by our recruitment team.***

title: Financial Crimes Compliance Analyst
description: The Role will report directly to the Head to Anti\-Financial Crime, Ireland and serves as a subject matter expert (SME) playing a critical role in safeguarding our European businesses against financial crime risks. Sitting within the second line Anti‑Financial Crime (AFC) function, this role carries significant responsibility in supporting Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services (FAS) business in the execution of the Financial Crime framework (which consists of AML and Terrorist financing, Sanctions, Bribery and Corruption and Tax Evasion) during the distribution of Vanguard UCITS range and oversight of Irish Fund's Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter\-Terrorist Financing (CFT) programme. The Role will report directly to the Head to Anti\-Financial Crime, Ireland and serves as a subject matter expert (SME) playing a critical role in safeguarding our European businesses against financial crime risks. Sitting within the second line Anti‑Financial Crime (AFC) function, this role carries significant responsibility in supporting Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services (FAS) business in the execution of the Financial Crime framework (which consists of AML and Terrorist financing, Sanctions, Bribery and Corruption and Tax Evasion) during the distribution of Vanguard UCITS range and oversight of Irish Fund's Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter\-Terrorist Financing (CFT) programme. **Core Responsibilities** * Support the maintenance and enhancement of the European AFC Framework, including methodology updates, documentation improvements, and alignment to group‑wide standards. * Advise the FAS business in respect to compliance with the Vanguard global financial crime policy requirements including local European obligations. * Create, update, and manage the Anti Financial Crime policies, procedures, and supplements. * In collaboration with the UK AFC team execute an assurance program for the areas that Vanguard Europe insources and support the creation of specific financial crime testing programs for oversight of outsourced third\-party relationships. * Review and analyse escalations in respect to due diligence, screening and transaction monitoring alerts. Resolves exceptions, including unusual and complex issues escalated by more junior team members, according to established guidelines. Collaborates with managers to resolve the most complex issues. * In collaboration with the UK AFC team execute of the Vanguard Europe annual risk assessments methodology for AML, Sanctions and Bribery and Corruption including detailed data gathering, quantitative and qualitative analysis, drafting of risk narratives, and coordination of validation and challenge with stakeholders across the First and Second line of defence. * Support the submission of periodic regulatory and internal AFC reporting for Vanguard Europe and the FAS business, ensuring all submissions are accurate, timely, complete, and delivered according to defined governance and regulatory expectations. * Assist project governance by helping prepare papers, progress updates, risk logs, and milestone reporting. * Provide formal and informal educational activities, including presentations, staff meetings and training programs. Develops and delivers financial crimes related training and education for Vanguard Europe crew members. * Maintains a comprehensive knowledge of the investment industry and compliance developments with implications for Vanguard's financial crimes program. Keeps abreast of the current market, regulatory and industry news. Serve as a consultant on compliance related issues. Acts as a liaison to regulatory agencies.Strong understanding of Anti Money Laundering/Counter Terrorist financing, sanctions and Anti Bribery and Corruption requirements, risk, regulatory expectations, industry practice, business systems and processes in Ireland and the UK. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Significant practical experience delivering or coordinating risk assessments, including data analysis, narrative writing, and validation stages. * Demonstrated experience managing regulatory or internal reporting cycles, including drafting, coordinating, and quality‑assuring submissions. * Experience working with policy governance, supporting policy refresh cycles, or maintaining standards frameworks. * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with experience drafting governance papers, risk assessments, or policy content. * High attention to detail, strong organisational skills, and ability to work under pressure in a fast‑paced environment. * Ability to interpret regulatory expectations and apply them pragmatically. **Desirable** * Experience operating across multiple European jurisdictions and/or global AFC structures. * Experience of supporting the distribution of an Irish UCITS range. * Exposure to transformation or change programmes (e.g., technology enhancements, operating model changes). **Qualifications:** * Minimum 5\+ years’ experience in AML/CFT, compliance, audit, or risk within financial services. Management Company/Asset Management 2nd Line AFC experience preferred. * Professional qualification relating to Financial Crime / Undergraduate degree or equivalent combination of training and experience **Special Factors:** * This position follows a hybrid working model that will require you to be in our Dublin office three days per week. * Vanguard is not offering visa sponsorship for this position. **How We Work** Vanguard has implemented a hybrid working model for the majority of our crew members, designed to capture the benefits of enhanced flexibility while enabling in\-person learning, collaboration, and connection. We believe our mission\-driven and highly collaborative culture is a critical enabler to support long\-term client outcomes and enrich the employee experience.

title: Financial Regulatory Solicitor, International Law Firm
description: Barden is working with an international law firm well established in Dublin who are looking to hire a Solicitor to join its highly reputed Financial Regulatory department. The firm has an excellent domestic and **international client base** in the financial sector, including banks, asset managers, broker\-dealers, trading platforms, insurers, payment services and FinTech companies. The Financial Regulatory team provides regulatory advisory and transactional services to the world's largest financial institutions, ranging from setting up businesses and launching new products and services, to dealing with regulators, advising on anti\-money laundering, financial sanctions, risk management, compliance frameworks, audits, investigations, etc. The organisation is known for promoting faster career progression than usual, with a very non\-hierarchical environment and very close access to the Partners. The firm is open to considering applications from Solicitors with financial regulatory experience in top law firms ranging from **3\+ years' PQE up to Of Counsel level** . They welcome exploratory and informal conversations with potential candidates who might be interested in hearing more about the opportunity and the team.

title: Financial and Regulatory Reporting Associate
description: **Job Description** Become a part of our Team and play a crucial role in our Financial \& Regulatory Reporting Team, maintaining our high standards and ensuring compliance across the board. As a Financial and Regulatory Reporting Associate within the Financial and Regulatory Reporting team you will focus on the regulatory reporting side of our services to JPMorgan clients. These services are high profile and vital to the continued development and success of JPMorgan's EMEA operations group. **Job Responsibilities** * Deliver service to clients ensuring that the preparation and review of regulatory returns in line with Central Bank of Ireland requirements. * Deliver regulatory and client reporting services including PRIIPS KIDs, EPTs, EMTs, TPT's (Solvency II), AIFMD (Annex IV Reporting), AIC MIR Reporting, CTI Reporting, UK ONS Reporting etc. to EMEA clients. * Manage any changes to existing regulatory reporting requested by the Central Bank of Ireland and other European regulators. * Ensure checklists and internal control guidance is followed during accounts \& returns production. * Operate within an appropriate control environment and continually assess the process/procedures to identify areas for improvement. * Work closely with Tech teams on the development of better solutions to prepare certain regulatory returns. * Act as primary contact for the regulatory bodies and ensure that queries/issues are resolved in a timely manner. Initiate, maintain and develop effective working relationships with external and internal customers and third\-party service providers. **Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills** * Strong communicator both externally and to all levels of business. Dedicated with strong attention to detail. * Effective escalation and resolution of issues. Ability to contribute to business as usual and projects in a time pressured environment. * Knowledge \& prior experience of regulatory reporting requirements for funds domiciled and sold within Europe. * Knowledge of financial instruments and their accounting treatment (e.g. Derivatives, Equities, Bonds). * Knowledge of funds types (e.g. ICAVs, Unit Trusts, Investment Companies, CCFs). * Proficient knowledge of software packages (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Adobe). * Ability to assist with writing of Ops Business Requirement Documents for Technology Teams as part of Project development. Ability to write Standard Operating Procedures for new services. **Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills** * Experience with data transformation tools is an advantage (e.g. Alteryx, Tableau, Xceptor, OWL DQ). * Experience of new client onboardings/onboarding of new services is an advantage. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world's most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **About The Team** J.P. Morgan's Commercial \& Investment Bank is a global leader across banking, markets, securities services and payments. Corporations, governments and institutions throughout the world entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. The Commercial \& Investment Bank provides strategic advice, raises capital, manages risk and extends liquidity in markets around the world.

title: Food & Beverage Assistant
description: **About Queensway** Queensway Group is a family\-owned business founded by entrepreneurs and renowned for its warmth, sincerity, and quality of service. We invest in real estate which we develop into operating business in the hospitality sector, from coffee shops to hotels, residential property, and a member’s club. The brands we operate include KFC, Starbucks, Point A Hotels, Montagu Place, Sloane Place, and the Sloane Club. With our entrepreneurial approach and extensive network, we can respond to opportunities and drive growth in our businesses. At Queensway we never stand still, and our agility is key to our success. This, together with our experience in real estate development, means we can effectively transform assets which are then operated by skilled and dedicated teams across the UK, Europe, and East Africa. **About Point A** At Point A we are defined by our state of mind, not the state of the market. When we work together, we believe anything is possible. Our strength lies in our ability to think differently and use what we have innovatively. We want to defy the status quo and challenge the leaders in our sector with our brand, products, and service. In everything we do, we are thoughtful, humble, honest, and true to our personal values. We deliver heartfelt hospitality to all our guests, no matter their budget. Since 2017 this approach has been key to our becoming one of the most successful new hotel brands, but we need fine folk to help us continue the story and write more of the successful paragraphs. **The Role** We are currently seeking **Food \& Beverage Host** team members to support our team and guests at Point **A Parnell Street** The role’s primary focus is on ensuring every single guest has an unforgettable experience at our hotel. **Key Requirements.** * Excellent customer service and problem\-solving skills. * Supporting other teams in the hotel and ensuring effective shift handovers. * Passion for service and delivering unforgettable experiences to our customers. * Positive attitude and drive. * Passion for developing yourself * Kindness, honesty and care for the surrounding community. * Good understanding of the industry and what it takes to be the best. * Imagination and ability to maximise business potential. Must have right to work in UK and be able to commute to property locations. **We will offer you:** * + Attractive salary, 40h a week contract. + Queensway Reward \& Recognition Scheme + Annual Awards Ceremony in London + Fun, friendly, and supportive work environment + Professional development and career progression + Career opportunities across the group including coffee houses and support office + Great team building initiatives and social events + Friends and Family Rates at all our properties + 25 days of annual leave **Salary: €15\.85 per hour**

title: Full time Kitchen porter
description: At **Gourmet Food Parlour** , we have an exciting opportunity for a hardworking, enthusiastic, **experienced kitchen porter** to join our team in our At **Gourmet Food Parlour** , we have an exciting opportunity for a hardworking, enthusiastic, **experienced kitchen porter** to join our team in our **Santry** based Catering Kitchen. title: Fund Accountant
description: **Accounting Analyst (Fund Accounting) \- Dublin / Hybrid** Join a global investment firm managing over $50bn in assets, operating a highly diversified hedge fund platform across hundreds of portfolio managers and strategies worldwide. Known for its innovative approach and cutting\-edge technology, the firm empowers teams to deliver consistent, risk\-managed returns for investors. **The Role** This **Accounting Analyst** role offers hands\-on exposure to fund accounting across multiple entities, with a unique opportunity to work closely with trading, operations, and investor relations in a fast\-paced hedge fund environment. Responsibilities * Complete month\-end close activities including journals, reconciliations, and financial reporting * Perform variance analysis across monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting cycles * Reconcile and analyse financial instruments including equities, fixed income, and derivatives * Liaise with fund administrators and support year\-end audits * Contribute to management reporting and continuous process improvement initiatives About You * 3\+ years' experience in accounting or finance (hedge funds, asset management, or practice preferred) * Strong analytical mindset with excellent attention to detail * Confident communicator with the ability to work across multiple teams * Proven ability to manage competing deadlines in a fast\-paced environment * Degree\-qualified in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline (strong academic record preferred) This **Accounting Analyst** opportunity is ideal for someone looking to deepen their fund accounting expertise within a high\-performing, collaborative environment. You'll benefit from working alongside experienced professionals, gaining exposure to complex financial instruments and a global investment platform. The role is initially onsite, with a transition to a hybrid working model after probation, alongside a competitive salary, strong benefits package, and a culture that values ownership, innovation, and teamwork. If you're ready to advance your career as an **Accounting Analyst** , apply today and take the next step in a world\-class investment environment.

title: Fund Accountant
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions and enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive at every stage of your career. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** A Fund Accountant is responsible for supporting the day\-to\-day accounting and financial reporting of investment funds (e.g., hedge funds, private equity, mutual funds). The role focuses on maintaining accurate financial records, assisting in Net Asset Value (NAV) calculations, and ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements. **Key accountabilities:** * Calculation of Net Asset Value (NAV) on a daily, weekly, or monthly basis * Record fund transactions (trades, income, expenses, fees) into the accounting system * Maintain and update general ledger accounts * Perform cash and position reconciliations with custodians/brokers * Investigate and resolve discrepancies (“breaks”) in fund records * Assist with internal controls and ensure accuracy of financial data * Process subscriptions, redemptions, and investor capital activity **Skills \& experience required:** * Strong attention to detail and accuracy * Analytical and problem\-solving skills * Strong Excel skills (data analysis, reconciliations) * Ability to work to tight deadlines (e.g. NAV cycles, month\-end) * Good communication and teamwork skills **Additional Information** * The role offers a hybrid / flexible schedule, which means there's an in\-office expectation of 3 or more days per week and the flexibility to work outside the office location for the other days. *This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship.* **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: Fund Accounting & Oversight - Associate
description: **About This Role** Elevate your career by joining the world's largest asset manager! Thrive in an environment that promotes positive relationships and recognizes outstanding performance! We know you want to feel valued every single day and be recognized for your contribution. At BlackRock we strive to empower our employees and actively engage your involvement in our success. With over USD $11 trillion of assets we have an outstanding responsibility: our technology and services empower millions of investors to save for retirement, pay for college, buy a home and improve their financial well\-being. Team Overview Position within the Alternatives Accounting and Product Controllers team being responsible for BlackRock’s Alternative's business. As an experienced professional, this position supports a complex and fast\-paced alternative investment fund business in a dynamic and challenging environment. Alternative Investments is a strategic focus for the firm and this position will be part of a core accounting team in Dublin, Ireland. The position offers significant growth opportunities both organically as the firm expands our product offerings, as well as opportunities to be involved in ongoing platform wide projects. Role Responsibilities * Demonstrate complete ownership of the day to day accounting and administrator oversight to ensure timely and accurate delivery of reporting to LPs * Interact with multiple internal and external partners over the course of the year, requires strong communication skills and ability to understand third party requests and deliver solutions to meet those partner needs * Ensure a robust control environment exists both at the fund service providers and internally to ensure risk of error is mitigated * Oversee regulatory compliance with SEC, NFA, and other regulations for product’s under the individual’s purview * Recalculate management fee and carried interest in accordance with fund legal documents and report associated revenue to corporate controllers team * Responsible for engaging with audit team around complex accounting topics and successful planning, coordination and completion of audited financials each year * Communicate performance results to investors and resolve investors’ questions. * Participate in investor due diligence meetings, as required for product set, and explain the controls in place to ensure accurate reporting to clients is achieved * Field custom reporting requests and projects for investors and/or business specific needs and develop reporting to deliver back to clients/partners * Drive increased efficiency and innovation through continuous review of operating procedures and planning / evaluating technology enhancements. **Experience** * 5\+ years of investment / fund accounting / fund operations / public accounting (audit) work experience or equivalent, including review experience for at least a portion of that time. * Bachelors’ degree in Accounting (strongly preferred) or Finance. CPA or equivalent a plus. * Excellent attention to detail and written and verbal communication skills * Proper and effective accounting skills including knowledge of GAAP, financial statements, performance fees and carried interest. * Sound analytical and problem solving skills. * Works effectively to plan and prioritize responsibilities including ongoing and ad hoc projects simultaneously; * Fluent in Microsoft Office products, emphasis on Excel. **Our Benefits** To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of employee benefits including: retirement investment and tools designed to help you in building a sound financial future; access to education reimbursement; comprehensive resources to support your physical health and emotional well\-being; family support programs; and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. **Our hybrid work model** BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. **About BlackRock** At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress. This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive. For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock \| Twitter: @blackrock \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected characteristics at law.

title: General Operative (Shift)
description: General Operative / Loop General Operative (Shift) **About SWARCO** At SWARCO, we shape the future of transport. As part of the global SWARCO Group, we develop cutting\-edge technologies for intelligent, sustainable, and efficient mobility ecosystems worldwide. From advanced traffic management to intelligent transport systems, our work reduces congestion, enhances safety, and minimises environmental impact. Join our collaborative team, innovating for smarter, greener cities globally. What you will do We are looking to recruit enthusiastic and motivated General Operatives / Loop General Operatives to join our Dublin field operations team. This is a hands\-on, physically demanding role working within a fast\-paced operational environment, supporting the installation, maintenance, and repair of Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) infrastructure across the road network. Working as part of a close\-knit team, you will help deliver safety\-critical roadside and motorway technology that keeps traffic moving safely and efficiently. The role involves a mixture of installation, maintenance, civil works, reactive fault response, and workshop preparation activities. You will work on a shift pattern supporting operational requirements and will play an important role in maintaining high standards of safety, workmanship, and customer service. Shift Pattern * Monday to Thursday: 17:30 \- 02:00 * Friday: 17:30 \- 01:00 Key responsibilities include: * Installing and maintaining ITS and CITS equipment to a high standard * Carrying out loop cutting, installation, and commissioning works * Attending faults and reactive maintenance activities appropriate to your training level * Assisting with installation and repair works on roadside technology equipment * Supporting civil works associated with operational and project requirements * Preparing, assembling, and testing equipment within the workshop environment * Carrying out lamping, cleaning, inspections, and routine maintenance activities * Completing site surveys and inspections * Ensuring all project paperwork, timesheets, job sheets, and documentation are completed accurately and returned on time * Maintaining company vehicles, tools, equipment, and PPE in a safe and serviceable condition * Supporting trainees, apprentices, and colleagues through guidance and knowledge sharing * Following all company Quality, Health \& Safety, Security and Environmental procedures at all times On a day\-to\-day basis, you will: * Work outdoors on live roadside and infrastructure environments * Support installation and maintenance activities across ITS projects and reactive works * Carry out physical and practical tasks including civil works and equipment installation * Work safely at heights and within live traffic environments * Ensure safe working practices and RAMS are followed at all times * Prepare and organise materials, tools, equipment, and vehicles for daily activities * Attend training courses and toolbox talks to support ongoing development * Work collaboratively with technicians, engineers, and operational teams to deliver successful project outcomes * Assist in achieving maintenance KPIs and operational targets , What we are looking for Essential: * Civils, construction, or infrastructure experience * Full B \& C driving licence * Machine or plant operator experience * Ability to work outdoors in a physical role * Ability to work at heights * Ability to work effectively as part of a team * Flexible approach to shift work, standby, and callout rotas * Strong safety awareness and commitment to safe working practices * SOLAS Safe Pass * Reliable, motivated, and professional attitude Desirable: * Electrical or electronic servicing training * Basic understanding of ITS site installations * Abrasive Wheels training * CSCS Signing, Lighting \& Guarding at Roadworks * CSCS Location of Underground Services * Previous experience within the traffic industry or similar field\-based environment * Good mechanical aptitude * Willingness to learn and take on new challenges , What we offer As well as providing a competitive salary and benefits package, SWARCO actively runs employee opinion surveys as part of our drive to provide a comfortable and supportive working environment. We are committed to supporting the ongoing development of our employees and creating long\-term career opportunities within the business. You will also receive: * Competitive salary of €42,000 \- €45,000 * 20% shift allowance * Structured training and development opportunities * Opportunity to work within a specialist ITS and transport technology environment * Supportive team culture and collaborative working environment * Company PPE, tools, and equipment * Career progression opportunities within SWARCO Interested? Please apply by clicking on the apply button. Applications are shortlisted on a rolling basis, and we reserve the right to interview and appoint before any advertised closing date. We therefore encourage you to apply at the earliest opportunity to avoid disappointment. Applications received after the closing date will not be processed. We have a responsibility to ensure that all employees are eligible to live and work in Ireland, therefore successful candidates must have the right to work in Ireland by the start of their employment. We are an equal opportunities employer. **\#ITS**

title: General Operative – Trainee Combi Jet-Vac Operative
description: Established in 1997, Super Drain is one of Leinster’s leading drainage and environmental contractors. We are recruiting entry\-level General Operatives to train within the drainage industry. Successful candidates will work alongside an experienced Senior Driver Operator as part of a two\-person crew on a specialist combi jet\-vac tanker. No previous drainage experience is required. Full on\-the\-job training will be provided for the right candidate. **The Role** Duties will include: * Assisting with commercial and industrial drainage cleaning * Operating high\-pressure jetting and vacuum equipment under supervision * Setting up hoses, tools, and equipment * Cleaning drains, gullies, tanks, grease traps, interceptors, and pipelines * Completing basic vehicle and equipment checks * Following all health, safety, and site procedures * Communicating professionally with clients and the Operations Team * Maintaining vehicles, equipment, and work areas **Requirements** Applicants must: * Hold a full, clean Category B driving licence * Have their own transport to travel to and from our Dublin 12 depot * Be flexible regarding overtime, early starts, on\-call duties, and travel * Have good written and spoken English * Be physically fit and comfortable working in drainage and waste\-management environments * Have excellent timekeeping, reliability, and customer\-service skills * Be willing to learn and work as part of a team Preferred but not essential: * Safe Pass * Manual Handling certificate * Confined Space certificate * Previous construction, drainage, waste\-management, mechanical, or trade experience * Category C licence and Driver CPC Training may be provided for suitable candidates. This is a permanent position following successful completion of the probationary period, with opportunities for further training and career progression. **No telephone calls will be accepted regarding this vacancy. Applications must be submitted through Indeed only and must include a CV and cover letter.** Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * On\-site parking * Sick pay Licence/Certification: * Any technical experience? (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: General Operative, South Dublin
description: **Location: Templeogue Dalkey** **Contract: Full Time Permanent** The Purpose if this role is to create and maintain a clean and hygienic environment for staff and residents throughout the service, including general areas, offices. To maintain the outdoor areas hygiene and upkeep the garden to include grass cutting and general garden maintenance. **What You Will Do** * Work within the framework of the overall aims and objective of Focus Ireland. * Work within the overall policies and procedures of Focus Ireland, inclusive of Health and Safety Policy. * Work within the guidelines of the daily routine, as set out by line management or by appointed systems. * Do a monthly stock check and keep stock records. To be responsible for requesting the ordering of cleaning and gardening materials in a timely manner as required in conjunction with the line manager. * Keep records and record information relevant to the NQSF cleaning programme requirements. * Work with other maintenance staff from Focus Ireland’s and to further cooperate with Contractors as agreed with the line manager when required. * Attend regular supervision and training as agreed with your line manager. * Clean offices, grounds, clean and prepare houses on and off site when required. * Report immediately any hazards or dangers to senior staff. * Report maintenance requirements to assigned staff. * Work within a team and co\-operate with fellow team members. * Attend team, divisional and organizational meetings as required. * Within reason and with prior notice, to be flexible in place and hours of work. * Work in adherence to Focus Ireland’s ‘Working Alone’ and ‘Confidentiality’ policy. * Undertake any other responsibilities and duties as may reasonably be assigned by your manager. **What You Will Need** * Good demonstrable verbal and written communication skills. * Ability to work alone, be self\-motivated and work as part of a team. * Proven previous experience in a similar role. * An understanding of the client group we work with in Focus Ireland. * Reasonable demonstrable I.T. skills. * Manual handling certificate (Desirable) * Chemical handling certificate (Desirable) **Focus Ireland is an equal opportunities employer** **\#FI3**

title: Graduate - Operations Technology Analyst
description: **Overview** The Operations Tech Analyst Programme (OA) is a 9\-month rotational programme for soon to be and recent graduates. This programme provides a blend of education, skill building, and exposure to Susquehanna and the financial services industry. It will prepare you for a full\-time position where you will be designing and developing applications and processes for the operations department. **Our Programme** As an Operations Tech Analyst, you will be exposed to the full life cycle of Susquehanna’s trading activity and develop a fundamental understanding of the industry and trading environment which Susquehanna operates in. Trading Operations work at the intersection of trading, technology, and external counterparties. You will work within the trading operations teams to decompose problems into their logical components and develop efficient technology solutions which will be released to the desks. You and the team will be responsible for the full lifecycle (Design, Build, Test and Maintain) of each application the Trading Operations Tech team takes ownership of. Working alongside our team you will have an in\-depth exposure to all the different trading desks and business areas at Susquehanna. Each rotation will incorporate hands\-on experience and real\-time tech projects to add value. Throughout the rotations you will work with our inhouse technology development teams as well as working closely with our tactical teams. If you have a keen interest in the financial markets, technology and collaborating with traders then this could be the role for you. **What’s In It For You** * Education: Susquehanna’s in\-house education programme is widely recognised as being the best in the industry. You will attend regular seminars to learn about in\-house business, products, and tech (Excel, SQL, Python etc.). * Support: You will be assigned a mentor from our trading operations team for each of your rotations. You will also be assigned a buddy who was a previous Operations Tech Analyst to guide you along the way. * Real Impact: You will develop solutions that will be immediately implemented by the teams. Here, you'll see the real impact that your problem\-solving skills can have on the firm. * Culture: Our non\-hierarchical culture allows employees of every level to thrive. We buck the trend in the Finance world. The environment is casual, collaborative and we focus on continuous development, no matter how long you’ve been in Susquehanna. * Perks: Based in the heart of Ireland’s Financial Services Centre, and a 10\-minute walk from Dublin’s city centre, our employees avail of free onsite catering, a games room, and a large in\-house gym, to name but a few of our favourite perks. **What We're Looking For** * Passion for Tech: You’re eager to apply your tech skills by working with Python, SQL, VBA or other programming languages where appropriate. This role is up to 80% coding so it is essential you have a flare for development work. * Problem Solver: You enjoy solving puzzles and love when you discover a solution to difficult problems. You’re not afraid to discuss ideas and work with others to find the optimal solution. * Communicator: You enjoy building relationships with those you work closely with. You enjoy sharing ideas, expressing your thoughts and listening to the views of others. * Ambitious: You constantly set yourself goals in life and give your all to achieve them. Be it in sports, music, academics, or another passion you have, you’re not afraid to put in the effort to reach your full potential. * Numerical \& Logical: When it comes to decision making, you use clear and sound reasoning. You’re rational and like to use numbers and data to come up with sensible solutions. * Interest in Finance: Interest in the financial markets and gaining exposure to a true front office trading environment. * Curious: You’re eager to find out more about what it’s like to work in the Finance sector, and specifically in an Operations role within a true front office environment. You have an identifiable interest in the financial markets and current affairs. * Soon\-to\-be or recent graduate: Our programme starts in early September 2026 **Interview Process** Apply \-\> Recruiter Interview \-\> Technical Interview \-\> Final Interview \-\> Offer Note: We hire for this programme on a rolling basis, therefore interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply early. **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Graduate Engineer - Wastewater
description: **The Vacancy** **About The Role** We are seeking a motivated and enthusiastic **Graduate Engineer** to join our Wastewater Engineering Team. This is an exciting opportunity for a recent graduate to kick\-start their career in civil or environmental engineering, working on a diverse range of wastewater infrastructure projects. As part of the team, you will gain invaluable hands\-on experience, develop your technical and project management skills, and work on projects that positively impact communities and the environment. You will have the opportunity to contribute to all phases of wastewater engineering projects, from feasibility studies to detailed design and construction, while learning from experienced engineers in a collaborative and supportive environment. You will also participate in our Graduate Programme, which provides structured training and mentoring throughout your first year. In addition to office\-based learning and development, the programme includes practical exposure through site visits and involvement in site\-based activities. **Key Duties And Responsibilities** * Preparation of Feasibility, Route, and Site Selection Reports. * Contributing to the preparation of Concept and Detailed Design Reports, including drawings, calculations, and specifications. * Developing Technical Specifications and Contract Documents for works and investigations. * Assisting in the preparation of documentation for statutory approvals, such as planning permissions and wayleaves. * Collaborate with senior engineers and project managers during construction stage contract administration. * Gaining exposure to developing detailed project plans, including budgets, schedules, and resource allocation. * Assisting in ensuring projects are delivered within budget, on time, and in compliance with regulations and standards. * Learning to identify and mitigate project risks under guidance from senior team members. * Performing site visits and inspections to monitor progress and quality. * Preparing and reviewing technical reports, drawings, and specifications with supervision. * Fostering effective communication and collaboration within the team, clients, and stakeholders. * Building your knowledge of wastewater engineering principles, regulations, and best practices. * Undertake additional responsibilities as required, in line with the evolving needs of the role and the business. **Key Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Civil or Environmental Engineering or a related discipline (or currently completing). * Interest in and basic understanding of wastewater engineering principles and practices. * Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to work effectively as part of a collaborative team and build positive working relationships. * Methodical, detail\-oriented approach to problem\-solving, with curiosity, a willingness to ask questions, and an eagerness to learn through mentorship. * Self\-motivated and dependable, able to take initiative on assigned tasks while contributing positively within a supportive team environment. * Familiarity with design software such as AutoCAD, Civil 3D, or similar tools is advantageous but not essential. **About Us** Our mission is to deliver sustainable solutions that transform communities and bring about lasting positive change. Through a blend of expertise, innovation and global dedication, we envision a world where sustainable engineering protects growing communities. Nicholas O’Dwyer provides professional services in engineering design, project management, environment and planning, construction supervision and technical and contract advisory. We are one of the most respected Irish consultancies operating in local and international markets, providing professional services to public\- and private\-sector clients. Whether in Ireland, the UK or internationally, we understand and solve major challenges associated with delivering and adapting infrastructure for an increasing global population while maintaining a sustainable environment for future generations. Nicholas O’Dwyer is part of the RSK Group, a leading integrated environmental, engineering, and technical services business providing dynamic, multidisciplinary services to a wide range of sectors. RSK was founded in 1989 and has grown considerably since then. At RSK, we operate as a group of businesses that work together seamlessly, aiming for growth through diversification. Our strategy helps us build a strong and resilient business, capable of weathering external challenges, while also providing greater value to our clients and more opportunities for our employees. Since the beginning, our philosophy has remained the same: employ, develop and reward talented individuals. People are crucial to our company, and we recognise that our success hinges on our colleagues' growth. We're committed to preserving RSK's people\-first culture, promoting innovation, and fostering a supportive and caring environment for collaboration. At RSK, we want you to be part of our successes and help you reach your career aspirations. We take pride in offering employees fulfilling and challenging careers by promoting ongoing growth and empowering individuals to excel. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that welcomes individuals from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We firmly believe that embracing and celebrating the diversity in our workforce makes us a stronger and more innovative company. If you're passionate about making a difference, join us as we work together to build a brighter, more equitable, and sustainable future for all. Do you have what it takes?

title: Graduate Loss Adjuster Programme (Ireland)
description: **Graduate Loss Adjuster Programme (Ireland)** **Start your career in casualty \& liability claims with Sedgwick** Are you a recent graduate looking for a career where no two days are the same? Want to build real expertise while helping people through challenging situations? Our **Graduate Loss Adjuster Programme** is designed to give you everything you need to become a confident, skilled casualty or liability adjuster. **What you’ll get** * **Structured training** * Learn the fundamentals of loss adjusting, including handling casualty and liability claims * **Mentorship from day one** * Be paired with an experienced adjuster who will guide and support your development * **Professional qualification support** * We’ll support you to achieve your **APA insurance qualification (January 2027 sitting)** * **Real experience** * Work on live claims and build practical skills from the start **What the role involves** As a Graduate Loss Adjuster, you will: * Support the management of **casualty and liability claims** from start to finish * Work with customers, insurers and third parties to investigate claims * Gather and assess information to understand what happened and what is covered * Help agree fair settlements * Keep accurate records and prepare reports * Build relationships with clients and stakeholders * Learn how to manage your own caseload over time You’ll split your time between **field visits** (homes or business premises) and **office\-based work** , developing both technical and people skills. **Why this role is different** * You’ll help people during difficult situations (e.g. injury claims or liability disputes) * You’ll develop **investigation, negotiation and problem\-solving skills** * You’ll gain a **clear career path in insurance and claims** * You’ll be part of a supportive, experienced team **Who we’re looking for** We’re looking for graduates who are: * Curious and eager to learn * Strong communicators (clear, confident and empathetic) * Organised and detail\-focused * Good problem\-solvers * Interested in building a long\-term career in insurance / claims **Requirements** * Degree in **Law, Business, STEM, Construction or Criminology** * Full **clean driving licence** (essential for site visits) * Willingness to travel across Ireland when required **Why join Sedgwick** * **Career growth** – clear progression and development opportunities * **Supportive environment** – mentorship and structured learning * **Meaningful work** – make a real difference to people and businesses * **Inclusive culture** – a workplace that values diversity and fairness

title: Graduate Program
description: **Launch Your Global Career After Graduation** **Teach English in China \| Full\-Time \| Entry\-Level Friendly** Just graduated (or graduating soon) and not ready for a desk job? Want to travel, gain international work experience, and build transferable skills while earning a competitive salary? **English 1** is hiring **recent graduates and early\-career professionals** to teach English in China.
